perturbation.original_text:
7. GENERAL
7.1. This agreement constitutes the whole agreement between the parties in relation to the subject matter hereof and no party shall be bound by any representations, warranties, undertakings or the like not recorded herein.
7.2. No addition to or variation, consensual termination or novation of this agreement, and no waiver of any right arising from this agreement or its breach or termination shall be valid or enforceable unless it is in writing a nd signed by all the parties or their duly authorised representatives.
perturbation.changed_text:
2. INTRODUCTION
2.1. The parties wish to present business proposals to each and/or wish to discuss and negotiate a future business relationship to their mutual benefit and that of their clients.
7. GENERAL
7.1. This agreement constitutes the whole agreement between the parties in relation to the subject matter hereof and no party shall be bound by any representations, warranties, undertakings or the like not recorded herein.
7.2. No addition to or variation, consensual termination or novation of this agreement, and no waiver of any right arising from this agreement or its breach or termination shall be valid or enforceable unless it is in writing a nd signed by all the parties or their duly authorised representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Entire Agreement' clause (7.1) and the 'Amendment' clause (7.2) are relocated to the 'Introduction'. This makes it ambiguous whether these general terms apply to the introductory statements themselves, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of any agreements or understandings not explicitly included in the formal body of the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_law: South African common law principle requiring contracts to clearly and unambiguously define the terms and conditions agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.law_citation: South African Contract Law - Principle of Certainty
perturbation.law_explanation: The principle of certainty in South African contract law dictates that contractual terms must be definite and leave no room for ambiguity. Moving essential clauses like the 'Entire Agreement' and 'Amendment' clauses to the introductory section creates uncertainty. It becomes unclear whether these clauses apply to the introductory statements, thus failing to provide a certain and unequivocal understanding of the agreement's terms.

perturbation.justification: The 'Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction' perturbation type specifies that a contradiction arises when a required legal disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section, making it difficult for parties to locate or interpret. The 'Entire Agreement' clause (7.1) and the 'Amendment' clause (7.2) being relocated to the 'Introduction' makes it ambiguous whether these general terms apply to the introductory statements themselves, creating uncertainty about the enforceability of any agreements or understandings not explicitly included in the formal body of the contract. This ambiguity weakens the clarity and enforceability of the contract, fitting the definition of a structural flaw leading to a legal contradiction, because it affects the clarity or enforceability and is placed in a misleading section of the contract.
